news.headline: Analysts cut iPhone sales forecasts due to AI delay, Apple stock falls; bulls lose confidence in upgrade cycle.
news.summary: Industry analysts have revised their projections for Apple iPhone sales this year, citing a delay in integrating artificial intelligence (AI) features. As a result, Apple's stock has experienced a decline in response to this update. This change has led to a decrease in confidence among investors regarding the iPhone upgrade cycle.

news.headline: ICON Energy Corp. reminds shareholders of 2025 AGM, receives Nasdaq notice due to share price< $1 for 30 days.
news.summary: In Athens, Greece, on March 13, 2025, Icon Energy Corp., a global shipping company traded on Nasdaq under the ticker symbol ICON, revealed that it received written notice from The Nasdaq Stock Market on March 7, 2025. The notice cited that due to the closing bid price of Icon's common shares being under $1.00 per share for 30 consecutive trading days (from January 23, 2025, to March 6, 2025), the company is now not in adherence with Nasdaq Listing Rule 5550(a)(2).

Wall Street bears take control, indices drop with Dow, S&P 500, and Nasdaq 100 losing 1.1%, 1.2%, and 1.7% respectively. Gold hits record high at $2,980/oz. Intel sees stock price increase due to CEO appointment. EU threat on tariffs on wine and spirits raises concerns.
news.summary: The bears took control on Wall Street as major indices erased gains from Wednesday and revisited recent lows amid investor worries about tariffs. Despite a much weaker-than-expected producer inflation report failing to lift risk assets, the Producer Price Index in February remained flat month-over-month, a significant drop from January's upwardly revised 0.6% and well below the 0.3% consensus estimate.U.S. President Donald Trump's threat to impose a 200% tariff on European wine and spirits imports unless the EU lifts its 50% tariff on U.S. whiskey added to the market unease. Safe-haven demand pushed gold prices to a record high of $2,980 per ounce, while major U.S. indices saw declines. The Dow, S&P 500, and Nasdaq 100 dropped by 1.1%, 1.2%, and 1.7% respectively, with all sectors facing pressure.

Nick Boulle to race at 2025 Mobil 1 Twelve Hours of Sebring for United Autosports USA on March 15 at Sebring Int Speedway.
news.summary: On March 13, 2025, it was announced that Nick Boulle, from de Boulle Diamond & Jewelry, will be racing with United Autosports USA at the Mobil 1 Twelve Hours of Sebring on March 15th. The event will take place at the Sebring International Speedway. de Boulle Diamond & Jewelry is a jewelry salon with locations in Dallas and Houston. This collaboration marks an important milestone in Boulle's racing career, and he is set to showcase his skills in this highly anticipated event.

Panasonic marks 25 years of their iconic FlashXpress™ Toaster Oven with launch of new Air Fry Toaster Oven and Anniversary Edition FlashXpress. Modernizing classic appliance with enhanced features.
news.summary: Panasonic has unveiled its latest kitchen appliances in Newark, N.J. on March 13, 2025. The new models, the FlashXpress™ Air Fry Toaster Oven NB-G211 Series and the FlashXpress Toaster Oven NB-G200, aim to bring modernized features and sleek design to classic kitchen appliances. The FlashXpress™ Air Fry Toaster Oven NB-G211 Series introduces enhanced functionality, while the FlashXpress Toaster Oven NB-G200 focuses on superior design. These cutting-edge appliances are set to revolutionize kitchen experiences with their innovative features.

news.headline: AI for Payment Integrity Summit discusses AI early intervention strategies to reduce costs and strengthen relationships.
news.summary: Healthcare payment integrity leaders are set to discuss the benefits of utilizing artificial intelligence to move towards earlier stages of payment verification processes, aiming to decrease expenses and enhance relationships with providers. The AI for PI Community, a top organization in healthcare payment integrity, will focus on this topic in Atlanta on March 13, 2025. This strategic shift towards AI is expected to deliver both financial savings and improved partnerships with providers.

news.headline: FireflySci raises prices due to new import tariffs, affecting cuvette scientific equipment for US labs.
news.summary: In Northport, New York on March 13, 2025, FireflySci, a top supplier of high-quality cuvette scientific research equipment in the United States, revealed new price changes due to recent import tariffs initiated by the previous administration. These adjustments will affect the cost of their products for laboratories nationwide.

news.summary: A recent report reveals that the unemployment rate in the country has reached a 10-year high, with 8.4% of the population currently unemployed. This represents an increase of 2.3% compared to last year. The report also highlights a significant decrease in job openings, with only 40% of the workforce currently employed. The government has announced plans to implement new initiatives to stimulate job growth and reduce the unemployment rate. Additionally, the report indicates that the GDP has decreased by 5.2% in the last quarter. Experts suggest that the economic downturn is likely to continue in the coming months.

Through ListAssist's natural language search feature, they aim to elevate experiences for agents and consumers alike. This development was announced in MURRAY, Utah on March 13, 2025.

news.headline: Facing US tariffs, should we retaliate or endure? This dilemma remains crucial in global trade.
RENERGEN starts selling liquid helium commercially, a significant milestone for the company.
news.summary: A recent report indicates that global carbon dioxide emissions have reached a record high in 2022, totaling about 39.9 billion tons. This increase marks a significant rise of 6% compared to the previous year. The spike in emissions can be attributed to the resurgence of economic activities post-pandemic, mainly driven by the industrial sector. The data also reveals that China remains the largest emitter of carbon dioxide, contributing around 10.4 billion tons, followed by the United States with 5.4 billion tons and India with 3.2 billion tons. Experts warn that urgent and robust actions are needed to curb these escalating emissions to combat climate change effectively.

news.headline: Circle to bring $900M money market fund under Bermuda DABA license, integrating with USDC for crypto exchanges. Global RWA tokenization market predicted to be $30T; Hashnote manages $900M in tokenized US Treasurys. Tokenization creates liquidity in illiquid markets. Music royalties could be next trend.
news.summary: Circle, the creator of USDC, is bringing its Hashnote Tokenized Money Market Fund (TMMF) under Bermuda regulatory oversight. Hashnote, acquired by Circle in January 2025, issues USDY, the largest tokenized treasury and money market fund with a TVL of $900 million, down from $1.9 billion in January. Circle plans to integrate USDY with USDC to enhance access between the TMMF and stablecoin. Bermuda's legal framework for digital assets allows for licensing, with Circle being the first firm to receive approval in 2021. Tokenized RWAs are seen as a $30-trillion opportunity globally, with the market cap for tokenized US Treasurys at $4.2 billion. Overall RWA market cap reached $15.2 billion in 2024, driven by institutional players venturing into various tokenization projects. Tokenization extends beyond assets like real estate, gold, and diamonds, transforming finance by enhancing liquidity and transparency through blockchain technology.

news.headline: Fuse Oncology, ACRO Partner to Integrate Clinical Pathways Platform
news.summary: In a press release dated March 13, 2025, Fuse Oncology disclosed a strategic collaboration with The American College of Radiation Oncology (ACRO). The agreement aims to streamline clinical pathways by implementing ACROPath®, a proprietary software. This partnership will have a significant impact on healthcare practices, potentially affecting numerous providers and patients in the field of oncology. The collaboration signifies a step towards standardizing treatment methods and improving the quality of care.

news.headline: Raoul Pal, Tom Lee believe Bitcoin could take significant share of $400-$500T global store of value market.
news.summary: Two experts, macro strategist Raoul Pal and Fundstrat co-founder Tom Lee, have conducted an analysis on Bitcoin's role within the global store value market estimated at $400 to $500 trillion. During a detailed discussion on March 13, Pal and Lee highlighted that Bitcoin is currently at a nascent stage of development and has the opportunity to attract a substantial share of the massive global store-of-value market. Lee, known for his insights on previous industry booms, echoed this sentiment. For further details, the complete article can be accessed on Benzinga.com.

Meta continued to show weakness, while Home Depot declined. Gold miners, on the other hand, made gains. The Dow Jones plummeted by 500 points due to a new tariff threat from Trump, and the S&P 500 saw a correction of 10%. Meta Platforms triggered a sell rule. This information is according to Investor's Business Daily.

news.headline: French wine and champagne makers devastated by Trump's tariff threat - 200% tax could cost France €4 billion in trade.
news.summary: Representatives from the industry have raised concerns about the potential consequences of imposing a 200 percent tax on European wines and spirits. They believe this tax could lead to a significant impact, potentially resulting in a cessation of shipments to the United States. Additionally, it is anticipated that this move could cause a detrimental impact on France's trade balance, resulting in a loss of €4 billion.

news.headline: Constellis achieved CMMC Level 2 certification by Cyber AB, vital for DoD compliance.
news.summary: In Herndon, Virginia, on March 13, 2025, the Cyber AB has granted Constellis certification at Cybersecurity Maturity Model Certification (CMMC) Level 2. This certification is crucial as it aligns with the Department of Defense's framework to safeguard companies handling Controlled Unclassified Information.

news.headline: Tampa General Hospital partners with Lakeside Medical Center in Palm Beach County to enhance healthcare access.
news.summary: In a recent development, Tampa General Hospital has entered into a partnership with Lakeside Medical Center to offer comprehensive strategic, operational, and administrative assistance. The agreement, finalized in early 2025, between Tampa General Hospital, located in Tampa, Florida, and the Health Care District of Palm Beach County, will see Tampa General extending its support to Lakeside Medical Center. This collaboration signifies a significant move in enhancing healthcare services in the region.

news.headline: Blend appoints Sara Pryor as Chief People Officer to lead people strategy, focusing on talent development.
news.summary: On March 13, 2025, in New York, Blend, a prominent provider of AI-driven data science solutions, revealed the establishment of the Chief People Officer (CPO) position. Sara Pryor has been selected to spearhead the company's human resources strategy. Pryor, with a wealth of experience in talent management, will be instrumental in shaping the company's workforce initiatives.

news.headline: Inventor from Texas creates COOL POCKET clothing to secure cold beverages; patented as CTK-1487.
news.summary: In Pittsburgh on March 13, 2025, an inventor from Nacogdoches, Texas, shared the inspiration behind the creation of the COOL POCKET. This innovative product offers a solution for keeping a cold beverage handy while working outdoors by storing it in a pocket or article of clothing. The inventor noticed a need for this convenient storage option and developed a design that effectively maintains the beverage's chill. This invention aims to enhance comfort and convenience for individuals who require easy access to cold drinks during outdoor activities.

news.headline: Anyware Robotics raises $12M in seed funding led by GFT Ventures, deploying Pixmo commercially.
news.summary: A company based in Fremont, California, known as Anyware Robotics, which specializes in multi-purpose mobile robotics with advanced intelligence, revealed on March 13, 2025, that they have successfully raised $12 million in seed funding. The funding round was primarily backed by GFT Ventures, along with contributions from Foothill Ventures and Black.

news.headline: Binance's CZ refutes claims of Trump family investing/pardon talks. WSJ reported discussions for Trump family to acquire a stake in Binance's U.S. branch.
news.summary: According to a recent report, representatives of the Trump family are currently in discussions with Binance to potentially acquire a stake in the cryptocurrency exchange's U.S. division. The Wall Street Journal highlighted this development, indicating significant interest from both parties. The involvement of the Trump family in this negotiation adds an intriguing element to the narrative surrounding Binance and its expansion in the United States.

news.headline: Robinhood adds PENGU, PNUT, POPCAT memecoins as it boosts crypto trading; sees 700% yearly rise in crypto revenues. Regulatory changes favor the industry.
news.summary: Robinhood recently listed memecoins Pengu (PENGU), Pnut (PNUT), and Popcat (POPCAT) in a move to expand its crypto offerings and compete with exchanges like Coinbase. The demand for memecoin trading surged, leading to a 700% year-over-year increase in crypto revenues in February. This comes as rival exchanges also accelerate new coin listings following US President Donald Trump's election win. Robinhood, traditionally a stock trading platform, has been investing heavily in crypto products. Trump's push to make America the "world’s crypto capital" and regulatory changes have also impacted the landscape. The US SEC recently stated that most memecoins do not qualify as securities, a shift from the Biden administration's stance. This regulatory change, along with rising market prices, has fueled increased activity in crypto trading.

news.headline: United States energy drink market forecast to reach $41.36 billion by 2033, growing at 7.99% CAGR due to high demand among young adults and athletes.
news.summary: A recent report titled "United States Energy Drink Market Size and Share Analysis - Growth Trends and Forecast Report 2025-2033" has been included in ResearchAndMarkets.com's offerings. It projects the United States energy drinks market to expand from US$ 20.71 billion in 2024 to US$ 41.36 billion in 2033, representing a CAGR of 7.99% during the period from 2025 to 2033. The surge can be attributed to the rising popularity of energy-boosting beverages among segments like young adults, athletes, and professionals. Continuous innovations in the sector also contribute to this growth.

news.headline: Three crypto firms, like Anchorage Digital, consult Treasury on safeguarding billions in Bitcoin reserve custody.
news.summary: Anchorage Digital, among other companies, is offering guidance to the U.S. government on strategies to protect crypto assets valued at billions of dollars.

news.summary: The S&P 500 is currently nearing a correction, with only a 10% decline from its peak in February. The uncertainty surrounding tariffs and trade negotiations continues to unsettle investors, leading to a volatile market environment.

news.summary: Monetate, a prominent personalization platform facilitating personalized digital connections between ecommerce brands and their worldwide customers, has introduced the Monetate Analytics Cloud in collaboration with Snowflake's Data Cloud. This new enterprise-level solution allows companies to effortlessly connect with Snowflake for direct access and analysis of their experimentation and personalization data. By integrating with Snowflake's Data Cloud, businesses can effectively eliminate silos and strengthen their measurement capabilities.

news.headline: Graybar reports record $11.6 billion in net sales for 2024, 100th anniversary celebration in 2025.
news.summary: In 2024, Graybar achieved a significant milestone with record-breaking net sales of $11.6 billion. The company is set to celebrate its 100th anniversary as an independent entity in 2025. Graybar, based in St. Louis, is a prominent distributor of electrical, industrial, automation, and connectivity products. This achievement positions Graybar as a key player in the supply chain sector, marking a successful century-long journey as an independent company.

news.headline: FTC requests delay in Amazon trial due to resource constraints from recent government cuts, later decides to meet court schedule. Staffing shortage cited.
news.summary: The Federal Trade Commission requested a delay in its trial against Amazon.com, Inc. (NASDAQ:AMZN) due to resource constraints caused by recent cuts from the Department of Government Efficiency. However, later the same day, the FTC decided to adhere to the court's schedule. FTC attorney Jonathan Cohen informed U.S. District Judge John Chun about the challenging situation, mentioning a reduction in staff within the agency, division, and case team, which he attributed to cost-cutting measures under the Trump administration. This change in stance came after initial concerns about meeting trial obligations due to limited resources. For more detailed information, please refer to Benzinga.com for the complete story.

news.headline: Goldman Sachs cuts FedEx forecast, lowers price target to $315 from $328. Analyst cites B2B weakness, tariffs, an industrial slowdown. EPS estimates for Q3 FY25 dropped to $4.40 from $4.65, and FY25 EPS estimates reduced from $18.85 to $18.65. FY26 EPS estimates lowered from $23.30 to $22.20.
news.summary: Analyst Jordan Alliger from Goldman Sachs has adjusted estimates for FedEx Corporation (NYSE:FDX) in anticipation of its third-quarter FY25 earnings report on March 20. Alliger, who rates the company as a Buy, has set a price target of $315, a decrease from the previous $328 forecast. In light of persistent transportation challenges, particularly in the industrial-exposed LTL sector, the analyst has decreased the third-quarter EPS estimates from $4.65 to $4.40. Moreover, Alliger has revised the FY25 EPS estimates downward from $18.85 to $18.65, attributing this adjustment to declines in volume within B2B parcel services such as U.S. Priority and Ground Commercial. Looking ahead to FY26, Alliger has reduced the EPS estimates from $23.30 to $22.20, primarily due to the expected decrease in B2B parcel volumes.
